title: L1.Quest 1: The First Spell
description: Write a program that prints the message "Welcome to CodeGuild, Apprentice!"
evaluationCriteria: The solution must correctly output exactly "Welcome to CodeGuild, Apprentice!" using standard console logging or printing.

title: L1.Quest 2: The Twin Potion Swap
description: Two variables A and B store values. Swap their values.
evaluationCriteria: The solution must demonstrate swapping two variables (e.g. using a temporary variable, or destructuring). It should output the swapped values correctly.

title: L1.Quest 3: The Loop of Endless Counting
description: Write a program that prints numbers from 1 to N.
evaluationCriteria: The solution must print all numbers from 1 to N cleanly using a loop. Ensure upper and lower bounds are inclusive.

title: L2.Quest 1: The Mirror of Strings
description: Determine whether a given string is a palindrome.
evaluationCriteria: The function should return true if the string is a palindrome and false otherwise. Code should handle edge cases like casing and single character strings.

title: L2.Quest 2: The Fibonacci Prophecy
description: Generate the first N numbers of the Fibonacci sequence.
evaluationCriteria: The solution must correctly generate the first N digits of the Fibonacci series starting from 0 and 1. Output the sequence cleanly.

title: L2.Quest 3: The Second Strongest Warrior
description: Find the second largest number in an array.
evaluationCriteria: Scan the array and identify the second largest value. Duplicates should be correctly ignored (e.g., [10, 10, 8] -> 8). Do not sort the entire array if possible to prioritize runtime.

title: L3.Quest 1: The Reversal Rune
description: Write a program that reverses a string.
evaluationCriteria: Must correctly reverse the characters of a string and handle empty strings. Cannot rely on basic built-in Array reverse if specified.

title: L3.Quest 2: The Frequency Oracle
description: Find the most frequent character in a string.
evaluationCriteria: Count the frequencies of all characters and return the one with the highest count. In cases of ties, return the first one encountered.

title: L3.Quest 3: The Guild Scroll
description:
Create a basic HTML webpage displaying:

Your Name
Guild Rank
Favorite Programming Language
evaluationCriteria: Provide valid HTML markup containing the specific structured information in semantic tags (e.g. h1, p, ul, etc.).

title: L4.Quest 1: The Hidden Message Toggle
description: Create a webpage where a hidden message appears when a button is clicked.
evaluationCriteria: The HTML must include a button and a hidden text block. Provide the JavaScript logic (event listener) to toggle the visibility of the text block.

title: L4.Quest 2: The Click Counter Relic
description: Create a webpage with a button that counts how many times it is clicked.
evaluationCriteria: The HTML must contain a button and text indicating the count. Provide JavaScript that initializes a state, adds an event listener to the button, and increments the displayed count on each click.

title: L4.Quest 3: The Stack of Forgotten Spells
description:
Implement a Stack with operations:
push()
pop()
peek()
evaluationCriteria: Implement a valid Stack data structure (class or functions). Must correctly handle pushing values, popping the top, and returning the top without removing via peek().

title: L5.Quest 1: The Coin Oracle
description: Given coin denominations and a target amount, find the minimum number of coins required.
evaluationCriteria: Must implement the minimum coin change algorithm perfectly using dynamic programming or BFS. Check edge cases like if change cannot be made exactly.

title: L5.Quest 2: The Path of Shortest Fate
description: Given a weighted graph, find the shortest path from a starting node to all other nodes.
evaluationCriteria: Implement Dijkstra's Algorithm or an equivalent robust shortest-path finder. Ensure correct behavior on loops and unreachable nodes.

title: L5.Quest 3: The Prefix Tree of Knowledge
description:
Implement a Trie (Prefix Tree) with the operations:
insert(word)
search(word)
startsWith(prefix)
evaluationCriteria: Correctly structure a Trie node class and Trie orchestrator. Methods must properly navigate character pathways. Ensure search vs startsWith handles full-word ending markers appropriately.
